subject: Geology
title: Back-Arc Basins
description: Learn Back-Arc Basins in one minute.

hook.text: Behind many island arcs, the crust stretches and thins, forming a small ocean basin nobody expected.

buildup.text: Back-arc basins form on the overriding plate behind a subduction zone where extensional forces stretch the crust.

discovery.text: The Sea of Japan and the South China Sea are both back-arc basins, formed by spreading behind volcanic arcs.

twist.text: These basins have their own mini seafloor spreading, producing new oceanic crust far from any mid-ocean ridge.

climax.text: Back-arc basins show that subduction doesn't just compress. It can also cause stretching and new crust behind the arc.

punchline.text: Subduction can stretch the crust behind it.

quiz.question: Where do back-arc basins form?
options: Behind subduction zones on the overriding plate
